content:
### 25Y Window Analysis
The **25Y window** features heavy clustering around **March 2026** (e.g., 2026-03-27 to 2026-03-12, correlations 0.928–0.644), with outliers in early 2022 (May 6–13, correlations 0.603–0.636), late 2020 (March 18–20, 0.608–0.617), 2025 (Jan 2/13, 0.636), 2023 (Oct 13, 0.616), 2024 (Dec 31, 0.609), 2021 (Sep 30/Oct 1, 0.603–0.616), and 2004 (May 17, 0.607). These suggest momentum profiles akin to recent volatility spikes amid Fed transitions and crises.

1. **MACRO REGIME**: Broad expansion in 2025 (S&P 500 +16.39% YTD Dec, Dow +12.97%), but cooling labor markets and ISM Manufacturing PMI at 48.7% (Oct 2025 contraction).[3] 2022 dates align with post-COVID inflation peak/contraction fears; 2020 March amid pandemic shutdowns.
2. **VOLATILITY**: Elevated intraday swings in 2025 (1.18% high/low vs. 0.91% in 2024); 2020 March saw extreme VIX spikes (implied by Dow point swings >1,000 pts).[1][3]
3. **SECTOR LEADERSHIP**: Recent rotation from AI/momentum (hyperscalers down) to value/energy/pharma/commodities (up 5% Nov–Dec 2025); 2022 May lagged tech amid unwind.[4]
4. **EARNINGS CYCLE**: Mid-Q1 2026 cluster post-Q4 2025 earnings; no major beats/misses noted, but AI-driven corporate strength through 2025.[5]
5. **FED & RATES**: Hawkish rhetoric in late 2025; Fed cut 25bps to 3.75%-4% Oct 2025 amid slowdown.[3][4]
6. **GEOPOLITICAL**: Oct 1, 2025 government shutdown (750k employees furloughed, programs halted); trade talks looming.[3][5]
7. **SENTIMENT**: Momentum unwind (long/short strategies -10% late 2025); breadth widening (493>7 stocks).[4]
8. **CREDIT CONDITIONS**: Hyperscaler capex/credit boom strained spreads; illiquidity from shutdown.[4]
9. **DOLLAR & COMMODITIES**: Commodities up (energy/refiners +5%); dollar likely firm on hawkish Fed.[4]
10. **WHAT HAPPENED NEXT**: Mixed; 2025 Apr dates show wild swings (e.g., Apr 9 +2,962 pts Dow, Apr 7/8 -300+ pts).[1] 2022 May stabilized post-unwind; 2020 March deepened before rebound.

### 5Y Window Analysis
The **5Y window** (2021–2026) mirrors 25Y top ranks but drops 2020/2004, adding 2022 May 11/16 (0.599–0.602) and 2025 Jan 10 (0.600). Focuses on post-2021 momentum akin to 2025–2026 volatility.

1. **MACRO REGIME**: 2025 expansion (S&P +16.39%) with labor cooling; 2022 May amid inflation tightening.[3][5]
2. **VOLATILITY**: 2025 intraday 1.18%; momentum unwind signals complacency shift.[3][4]
3. **SECTOR LEADERSHIP**: Rotation to value/energy (+5%) from AI/crypto (-20%+ Nov–Dec 2025); 2022 lagged tech.[4]
4. **EARNINGS CYCLE**: Q1 2026 cluster post-2025 AI-fueled earnings strength.[5]
5. **FED & RATES**: 2025 hawkish turn after Oct cut; yields likely rising.[3][4]
6. **GEOPOLITICAL**: 2025 shutdown, tariffs/demographics headwinds.[3][5]
7. **SENTIMENT**: Unwind nearing end (eighth inning); active/value outperforming.[4]
8. **CREDIT CONDITIONS**: Tight from capex boom/shutdown illiquidity.[4]
9. **DOLLAR & COMMODITIES**: Commodities firm; USD supported.[4]
10. **WHAT HAPPENED NEXT**: 2025 Apr volatility (e.g., Apr 9 rally, prior drops); 2022 May breadth improved short-term.[1][4]

### 1Y Window Analysis
The **1Y window** (March 2025–2026) emphasizes **March 2026** cluster (correlations 0.928–0.345), plus April 2025 (Apr 4/7/8/10/16/17, 0.487–0.357) and Nov 2025 (Nov 20/21/24/25, 0.329–0.376). Lower correlations indicate scarcer recent matches, tied to 2025 turbulence.

1. **MACRO REGIME**: 2025 late-year slowdown (ISM 48.7%, unemployment stabilizing <4.5% into 2026).[3][5]
2. **VOLATILITY**: High 2025 swings (Dow Apr 7–10: 1,500+ pt ranges).[1][3]
3. **SECTOR LEADERSHIP**: Nov 2025 rotation from speculative (crypto -20%) to commodities/pharma.[4]
4. **EARNINGS CYCLE**: Post-Q4 2025; April 2025 mid-Q1 volatility.[5]
5. **FED & RATES**: Hawkish post-Oct cut.[3][4]
6. **GEOPOLITICAL**: Shutdown fallout into Nov; trade tensions.[3][5]
7. **SENTIMENT**: Momentum unwind, breadth expansion.[4]
8. **CREDIT CONDITIONS**: Strained by capex/shutdown.[4]
9. **DOLLAR & COMMODITIES**: Energy/commodities leading.[4]
10. **WHAT HAPPENED NEXT**: Apr 2025 extreme (Apr 9 +7.3% Dow close, but prior -0.9% to -2.6%); Nov 2025 led to Dec minor dip (S&P -0.05%).[1][3]

### Cross-Window Synthesis
**Common themes**: All windows dominated by **March 2026 momentum** amid 2025–2026 volatility spikes, Fed hawkishness post-cuts, sector rotation (away from AI/momentum to value/commodities), government shutdown fallout, and credit strains from capex/illiquidity. Elevated volatility (1.18% intraday 2025) and sentiment unwinds recur.[1][3][4]

**Key differences**: 25Y includes crisis outliers (2020 COVID crash, 2004 pre-boom, 2022 inflation), broadening to deeper contractions; 5Y trims to post-COVID (2021–2022); 1Y narrows to 2025 volatility (Apr/Nov clusters), with weaker correlations signaling unique recent regime (AI exuberance fading).[1][4][5]

**Best synthesis**: Pattern suggests **choppy consolidation or mild pullback over next 5–10 days**, followed by potential momentum snapback. Historical analogs (esp. 2025 Apr) show intraday extremes resolving in net gains after unwinds (e.g., +4.89% Dow days), but hawkish Fed/shutdown risks cap upside amid rotation. Expect 1–2% S&P swings, favoring value/energy over tech, with breadth support limiting downside.[1][3][4]
